grant_title: Institutional Training Funding Considerations and Contacts
geographic_restrictions: No geographic restrictions, but NIGMS strongly encourages applications from IDeA-eligible states, HBCUs, and Tribal Colleges and Universities.
project_area: Biomedical research training across various NIH institutes, including clinical and translational science, cancer research, vision science, genomics, alcohol-related research, musculoskeletal and skin diseases, biomedical imaging and bioengineering, child health and human development, drug abuse research, dental and craniofacial health, diabetes and digestive diseases, environmental health sciences, general medical sciences, neurological disorders, and veterinary research.
demographic_requirements: No specific demographic requirements, but programs are encouraged to recruit and retain diverse trainees, including underrepresented groups in biomedical research.
eligibility_summary: Eligible applicants include institutions of higher education, research institutions, HBCUs, Tribal Colleges and Universities, and resource-limited institutions. Programs must align with the mission and interest areas of the specific NIH institute or center. Training programs should prepare trainees for careers in biomedical research, with a focus on predoctoral, postdoctoral, and clinical fellows, depending on the institute's priorities. Some institutes have specific trainee slot limits or project period durations.
